meta.title: AllyOS Peptides — Provider CDS Operating Module
meta.source: Consolidated from the AllyOS locked peptide data layer: peptide-packs.json (grade->tier + consent), contraindications.json (per-agent absolute/relative), peptide-interactions.json (cross-agent rules), monitoring-map.json (lab/vital monitoring), intake-schema.json (override policy + red-flag screen). This module is the distilled OPERATING layer; the component files remain the deep reference.
meta.curated_by: Armando A. Falcon, MD (FL ME 84789) — RenuviaMD® PLLC, AllyOS Compliance Division
meta.scope: Provider decision-support for a cash-pay peptide program — metabolic (GLP-1/incretin backbones + cyclable adjuncts) and the FDA-approved peptide agents with a label. Grade->consent-tier matching, red-flag/GLP-1 screen, absolute/relative contraindications, cross-agent interaction flags, and monitoring. NOT a product list, NOT a recommendation to use any agent, NOT autonomous prescribing.
meta.honesty_discipline: Provider-facing only; never patient-facing output. No doses (decision-support, not a prescription). FDA-approved agents (GLP-1s, tesamorelin, PT-141/Vyleesi, SS-31/FORZINITY) carry LABEL-established contraindications. ALL OTHER agents (research/compounded/investigational) have NO formal label list — their absolutes are prudent-convention and relatives are mechanism-based, each marked VERIFY pending Medical Director sign-off. No empiric/anti-aging/longevity efficacy claims. The provider confirms and prescribes every decision.
meta.phi_posture: PHI stays local (clinic/provider). AllyOS holds no PHI; only the de-identified decision marker flows to MD oversight. Consent/intake are generated on-device via the Visit Packet under the clinic's own HIPAA responsibility.

output_rules.provider_only: Outputs only provider-facing clinical decision-support. No patient education, marketing claims, or 'dear patient' language. No doses.
output_rules.no_source_response: NO VERIFIED LABEL/SOURCE for this agent/claim — present as VERIFY, provider review required; do not state as established.
no_empiric_pathway: GH peptides build muscle as claimed

prescriber_authority.rn_staff: RN/MA/coordinator may assist with intake, vitals, med reconciliation, lab collection/upload, scheduling, routing, and provider-approved education. They may NOT diagnose, determine candidacy, select an agent, prescribe, authorize refills, change doses, interpret labs for medication decisions, clear contraindications, or clear red flags.
prescriber_authority.block_message: BLOCKED — peptide medication decision requires an authorized APP/physician provider of record. RN/staff support intake and routing only.

consent_tiers.2: Enhanced — FDA-approved off-label / Grade B (2–3 pg, off-label disclosure)
consent_tiers.3: Comprehensive experimental — Grade C / investigational / not-FDA-approved (4–6 pg, experimental attestation)
consent_tiers.4: Maximal — Grade D, generally not recommended (6–10 pg, maximum disclosure)

grade_to_tier.rule: Consent tier = the agent's matched tier. A stack's tier = the HIGHEST component tier. Investigational/not-approved forces Tier 3 even if trial-grade is B (e.g. retatrutide).

metabolic_red_flag_screen.note: One shared metabolic/GLP-1 red-flag screen covers every metabolic-line agent (authored once, reused).
hard_screen: Personal/family history of MTC (medullary thyroid carcinoma) — boxed warning
screen: Severe gastroparesis / severe GI motility disease
Type 1 diabetes / DKA history (GLP-1 is not insulin and not for T1DM)
Significant kidney disease (e.g. GFR<30) / volume depletion
metabolic_red_flag_screen.glp1_indication_threshold: BMI >=30, or 27–29.9 + a weight-related comorbidity (Grade A, FDA Wegovy/Zepbound labels).

glp1_rules.one_backbone_only: Exactly ONE GLP-1/incretin backbone at a time. Never run two GLP-1/incretins concurrently — they overlap mechanistically and are continuous, not cycled. To change agent, use the TRANSITION / switch protocol, do not co-administer.
glp1_rules.continuous_not_cycled: GLP-1/incretin backbones are continuous (chronic, titrated), not cycled.
glp1_rules.stack_rule: Weight-loss stack = 1 GLP-1 backbone + 1–2 of {sermorelin, AOD-9604}. Metabolic stack = <=1 GLP-1 backbone + 1–3 cyclable adjuncts {sermorelin, AOD-9604, MOTS-c, SS-31}, total <=3 agents. Selecting a second GLP-1 routes to the Transition Protocol.
glp1_rules.pregnancy: GLP-1/incretins contraindicated in pregnancy; discontinue ~5 half-lives before planned conception (Grade A).

formulary.note: Stocked/operational agents. evidence_status: FDA_LABEL = label-established; VERIFY = prudent-convention/mechanism-based pending MD sign-off. No doses.

glp1_backbones.name: Semaglutide
relative: Pancreatitis hx (d/c if suspected; do not restart if confirmed)
Severe gastroparesis / severe GI motility disease
Volume depletion / CKD (AKI from GI losses)

glp1_backbones.name: Tirzepatide
absolute: Serious hypersensitivity to tirzepatide or excipients
relative: Acute/recent pancreatitis (d/c if suspected; do not restart if confirmed)
Pregnancy/planning (fetal harm; reduced oral-contraceptive efficacy — barrier/non-oral 4 wk after start & each dose increase)
Diabetic retinopathy (monitor with rapid glycemic improvement)
monitoring_followup: A1c, Fasting glucose, Renal function/eGFR (3 mo, 6 mo + every 3–6 mo)

glp1_backbones.name: Liraglutide
absolute: Serious hypersensitivity to liraglutide or excipients
relative: Pancreatitis hx (d/c if suspected; do not restart if confirmed)
monitoring_followup: A1c, Fasting glucose, Renal function/eGFR (6 mo + every 3–6 mo)

glp1_backbones.name: Retatrutide
absolute: (VERIFY) Personal/family MTC or MEN 2 — class signal, not label-established
relative: (VERIFY) Tachyarrhythmia / uncontrolled arrhythmia / decompensated HF / ischemic heart disease — dose-dependent HR rise; baseline HR/ECG if cardiac risk
(VERIFY) Glucagon agonism may raise glucose/hepatic output (caution brittle diabetes)
monitoring_followup: BP/HR (standard GLP-1 continuous cadence: baseline, ~4 wk, 3 mo, 6 mo, ongoing)
glp1_backbones.monitoring_note: Source keys all items 'followup'; no discrete baseline-tagged item despite the cadence text — provider draws baseline per the cadence.

cyclable_adjuncts.name: Sermorelin
cyclable_adjuncts.fda_status: off-label (Geref was FDA-approved for pediatric dx; adult wellness use is off-label)
absolute: (VERIFY) Non-functional pituitary / disrupted HP axis (won't respond)
relative: (VERIFY) Concurrent glucocorticoids may blunt GH response

cyclable_adjuncts.name: AOD-9604
relative: (VERIFY) Active malignancy — precautionary, weak GH-axis rationale

cyclable_adjuncts.name: MOTS-c
relative: (VERIFY) Active malignancy — precautionary, mixed preclinical signals
(VERIFY) Diabetes/glucose-lowering agents (AMPK/insulin sensitization; theoretical additive hypoglycemia; monitor glucose)

cyclable_adjuncts.name: SS-31 (Elamipretide)
cyclable_adjuncts.fda_status: Elamipretide FDA-approved (FORZINITY, Barth syndrome 2025); compounded metabolic use is off-label/investigational
cyclable_adjuncts.evidence_status_detail: on-label elamipretide/FORZINITY = FDA_LABEL; compounded metabolic/wellness use = INVESTIGATIONAL
absolute: Serious hypersensitivity to elamipretide or any excipient (FORZINITY label)
relative: Hypersensitivity incl. serious allergic reactions (d/c and seek care)
SC only — not approved for IV
Benzyl-alcohol excipient NOT for neonates (fatal gasping syndrome; avoid in pregnancy/neonates absent clear need)
(VERIFY) Active malignancy — precautionary in compounded longevity use, not a label CI
(VERIFY) Compounded SS-31 vs FORZINITY — purity/benzyl-alcohol/dosing unverified
cyclable_adjuncts.monitoring_note: No lab monitoring by design — functional/symptom/screen-based (in agents_without_lab_monitoring).

fda_approved_other.name: Tesamorelin
fda_approved_other.consent_tier_note: off-label anti-aging use; VERIFY at lock
fda_approved_other.fda_status: FDA-approved (EGRIFTA) for HIV lipodystrophy; off-label anti-aging not label-covered
fda_approved_other.evidence_status_detail: on-label (EGRIFTA, HIV lipodystrophy) = FDA_LABEL; off-label anti-aging use = PRUDENT_CONVENTION_VERIFY
absolute: Disruption of HP axis (hypophysectomy, hypopituitarism, pituitary tumor/surgery, head irradiation/trauma)
Active malignancy (must be inactive & treatment complete before start)
Known hypersensitivity to tesamorelin or excipients (e.g. mannitol)
relative: IGF-1 elevation (monitor; consider d/c with persistent elevation)
Glucose intolerance / new or worsening diabetes (monitor)
Acute critical illness (consider d/c — GH-class mortality signal)
fda_approved_other.note: Not in the metabolic_line of peptide-packs; tier shown is the off-label convention — VERIFY at lock.

fda_approved_other.name: Bremelanotide (PT-141 / Vyleesi)
fda_approved_other.consent_tier_note: Tier 1 on-label (HSDD); Tier 2 if used off-label — VERIFY
fda_approved_other.fda_status: FDA-approved (Vyleesi) for HSDD in premenopausal women
absolute: Hypersensitivity to bremelanotide or any component
relative: High CV risk — not recommended (assess CV risk, control BP)
(VERIFY) Oral naltrexone-containing products — may significantly decrease naltrexone levels, not recommended
Focal hyperpigmentation (face/gingiva/breasts; more with daily dosing/darker skin; may not resolve)
monitoring_baseline: BP/HR (BP + CV-risk gating; oral-naltrexone review; pregnancy)

interaction_rules.note: Cross-agent (peptide-peptide) flags. 31 of 32 source rules fire (involve >=1 stocked agent); only IR022 (oxytocin+vasopressin, both non-stocked) does not. Actions: FLAG_FOR_PROVIDER_REVIEW (surface for review, never auto-block), INFORM (context), LANGUAGE_GUARDRAIL (force precise wording). 'direct' = direct_combination_evidence. Decision-support only — no doses, never a hard block.

key.rule: GLP-1 receptor-agonist redundancy {semaglutide, liraglutide, tirzepatide, retatrutide}
key.why: Core 'never two GLP-1' enforcement; high mechanistic, low direct clinical for stacking.
GI / heart-rate overlap; no direct evidence.

key.rule: GLP/amylin GI slowing + absorption-sensitive oral agents
GHRH analog + GHSR agonist GH synergy {sermorelin, tesamorelin, CJC-1295, ipamorelin, GHRP}
GH/IGF axis vs insulin sensitivity {GH-axis + MOTS-c, GLP-1s}

key.why: Do not call AOD a GH secretagogue.
Convergent metabolic nodes, not one booster.
Distinct nodes, not a single 'mito booster'.

coverage_gaps: cjc-ipamorelin-blend (present in other files; no stocked interaction node)

override_policy.principle: The provider can override ANY recommendation. AllyOS is decision-support, never a gatekeeper. Every override is a documented clinical decision by the licensed provider — which is what keeps the platform a tool and transfers responsibility to the provider.
override_policy.hard_stop: blocking modal requiring a TYPED override rationale; recorded in the note + consent. Highest friction, still overridable — not an absolute lock.

governance.provider_of_record: Every active peptide patient must have an authorized APP/physician provider of record before any prescription/refill/dose-change is finalized. Block if missing or if the named prescriber is RN/LPN/MA/coordinator/unlicensed.
governance.medical_director: Clinic governance/QA oversight unless separately contracted as treating provider. Approves the framework, evidence rules, audit criteria; reviews high-risk/AE/compounded/investigational charts; performs/supervises the monthly QA audit. Does NOT automatically include routine prescribing/direct-care.

risk_score.5: Mandatory MD review — adverse event, research-chemical/unverifiable source, RN/staff as prescriber, GLP-1 red flag (MTC/MEN2, pancreatitis) not screened, missing provider order, two GLP-1 co-administered
risk_score.4: High — new start, compounded/off-label or investigational agent (Tier 3–4), interaction flagged, source/compounding concern, abnormal lab needing review
risk_score.3: Moderate — stable continuation, routine lab review, documentation correction
risk_score.2: Routine — stable maintenance, complete documentation, no change/concern
risk_score.1: Low — administrative update or discontinued agent

audit.shared_spine: Identical §14 risk spine as the IV and BHRT modules; the de-identified marker flows to MD oversight.

source_map.purpose: Binds each operating element to its source layer. FDA_LABEL = label-established; VERIFY = prudent-convention/mechanism pending MD sign-off.
source_map.fda_other_label_contraindications: contraindications.json (EGRIFTA tesamorelin; Vyleesi bremelanotide; FORZINITY elamipretide)
source_map.research_agent_contraindications: contraindications.json — prudent-convention/mechanism, marked VERIFY pending Falcon sign-off
source_map.monitoring: monitoring-map.json (provenance-locked to each agent's own monitoring text)

final_rule: Ally supports the provider. Only the authorized APP/physician provider of record makes peptide medication decisions and prescribes. RN/staff support intake and routing only. No doses are issued by AllyOS. FDA-approved agents carry label-established contraindications; all research/compounded/investigational items are VERIFY pending Medical Director sign-off. No source = no recommendation.

signoff_ledger.status: DRAFT — consolidated from the locked peptide data layer; pending 3-auditor lock + MD sign-off
signoff_ledger.citation_posture: FDA-approved agents (GLP-1s, tesamorelin, PT-141/Vyleesi, SS-31/FORZINITY) are LABEL-grounded. Research/compounded/investigational agents carry prudent-convention/mechanism contraindications explicitly marked VERIFY — these require Dr. Falcon's clinical sign-off before they are presented as established (the source contraindications.json carries the same VERIFY posture).

auditors.clinical: PASS-WITH-NOTES (2026-06-28) — consolidation clinically faithful; boxed warnings, one-GLP-1/transition rule, platform hard stops, grade->tier, VERIFY discipline, no-dose all correct. Notes (faithful-to-source, disclosed): pregnancy placement non-uniform across GLP-1s (per labels); retatrutide has no baseline-tagged monitoring item in the data layer (disclosed via monitoring_note) — fix belongs at monitoring-map.json lock.
auditors.citation_regulatory: PASS-WITH-NOTES (2026-06-28) — no false FDA-approval claims (retatrutide correctly investigational), fully source-bound, advisory-only interactions (no auto-block), no doses/no-sell, honest DRAFT posture. Schema-hygiene notes APPLIED (see schema).
auditors.schema: PASS-WITH-NOTES (2026-06-28) — strict JSON, 10 unique slugs, IR001-IR032 complete (31 fire, IR022 not). Notes APPLIED: consent_tier normalized to integer everywhere (+ consent_tier_note), evidence_status normalized to a single enum token (+ evidence_status_detail) for ss-31/tesamorelin; bare non-enum 'VERIFY' removed.

signoff_ledger.lock_status: 3-auditor lock COMPLETE — all PASS-WITH-NOTES, render/schema notes applied. The module is structurally locked and clinically faithful. It remains DRAFT on purpose: unlike BHRT (machine-verifiable citations), the research/compounded/investigational agents carry prudent-convention (VERIFY) contraindications that require the Medical Director's CLINICAL judgment to clear — that is the sign-off step.
pending: Medical Director clinical sign-off (Falcon, MD) — review/clear or correct each (VERIFY) research-agent item, then set meta.effective + flip status to locked. FDA-label agents (GLP-1s, tesamorelin, PT-141, SS-31/FORZINITY) are already label-grounded.
